# Goal 3 Tasks

Status legend: `[ ]` pending, `[~]` in progress, `[x]` complete.

Rules:

- Execute only the first unfinished task in each continuation.
- At the start of every continuation, read `goal-3/input.md`, `goal-3/plan.md`, and this file in full.
- Each task must be independently verifiable.
- Every third task is a large comprehensive check-debug loop.
- Every completed task must fill its completion record: work done, verification evidence, residual risk, next step, and commit id if a commit was created.
- Do not modify code before the Goal 3 initialization files exist.
- Commit code changes after each completed task when tracked product files were changed and verification supports the change.

## Task 5: StatementDiffGate And Statement Drift Red Team

- [x] Implement or harden `StatementDiffGate` for exact theorem header/type hash matching by default.
- [x] Reject weakened, strengthened, different, ambiguous, wrong-domain, wrong-quantifier, or hidden-assumption candidate statements.

Completion record:

- Work done: added `StatementDiffGate` with exact theorem header/type/hash default matching, explicit hard vetoes for statement drift, weakening, strengthening, ambiguous equivalence, hidden assumptions, wrong domain, wrong quantifier, and non-exact equivalence without Lean replay evidence. Added a Statement Drift Red Team report helper that aggregates gate findings and unresolved counterexample hard vetoes. Hardened `decision-forest` so `statement_equivalence_claim: "equivalent"` is not proof-grade unless candidate evidence carries Lean equivalence replay material, and propagated hard vetoes into both the ensemble decision and gate decision. Added `goal3-task5-statement-diff-gate.test.mjs` and wired it into the default `@comath/comathd` test chain.
- Verification evidence: TDD RED was observed before implementation: `node services/comathd/tests/unit/goal3-task5-statement-diff-gate.test.mjs` failed because `../../dist/index.js` did not export `createStatementDriftRedTeamReport`. Added `goal3-task102-final-global-replay-authority-producer.test.mjs`, which uses project-local fake `lean`/`lake` binaries through the service-owned spawn path, not injected replay callbacks. Implemented a `final_global_replay` success branch gated by `.comath/campaign/<campaign>/final_replay_request.json`; the branch runs `runCleanLeanReplay()`, packages final authority v3 evidence with derived bindings, sets claim gate prerequisites only after clean replay and packaging pass, promotes through the ordinary claim gate, writes `formal_replay_authority_evidence`, and then marks the campaign `completed_formal_proof`. No request or incomplete evidence still produces the existing replayable blocker. `runCleanLeanReplay()` now returns v3 authority paths, records a `purpose: "final_replay"` LeanRunManifest, includes FormalSpecLock/AssumptionLedger in clean input hashes, writes the third-party replay pack path, and binds Lean/Lake executable hashes into both LeanRunManifest v3 and FinalReplayManifest v3.

Implemented accepted-artifact assembly for `comath.campaign_final_replay_target.v1`: it reads `campaign.accepted_artifacts`, recomputes the artifact SHA-256, requires matching project/campaign/root claim, requires `artifact_role: "final_replay_request_source"`, `proof_authority: "none"`, `can_promote_claim: false`, requires an exact root-claim `obligation_id`, validates campaign-native replay scope with no `task_id`, then writes `.comath/campaign/<campaign>/assembled_final_replay_request.json` only after the replay request parses and binds to the expected obligation. Task104 moves final replay request assembly from a fixed request file to accepted campaign/workstream artifacts, but it still depends on an accepted artifact already containing the replay request payload. It does not yet make earlier native campaign stages plan and emit that replay target automatically, install/configure Lean, fetch mathlib, broaden theorem coverage, provide OS-level sandboxing, validate full Pi/Codex lifecycle behavior, or complete final GA audit.

Completion record:

It skips production when an explicit request or already accepted target exists, reads only accepted artifact-store material, recomputes artifact hashes through the existing accepted-artifact reader, requires exact project/campaign/root-claim/obligation binding plus `artifact_role: "final_replay_material_source"`, `proof_authority: "none"`, and `can_promote_claim: false`, constructs a campaign-native replay request with no `task_id`, validates it through the same parser used by Task104, writes `.comath/campaign/<campaign>/generated_final_replay_target.json`, imports that generated target through `importArtifact()`, and appends the returned `ArtifactRef` to `campaign.accepted_artifacts` before advancing to `final_global_replay`.

After implementation and `corepack pnpm --dir services/comathd build`, Task105 exited 0 and asserted that no fixed `final_replay_request.json` or prebuilt replay target was present, `final_static_audit` generated and imported `comath.campaign_final_replay_target.v1`, the target retained source material artifact id/hash/path provenance, the next tick completed Lean Authority v3 clean replay, root claim became `formally_checked`, and export reported `lean_kernel_clean_replay`. Focused adjacent regressions exited 0: `node services/comathd/tests/unit/goal3-task104-accepted-artifact-final-replay-request.test.mjs`, `node services/comathd/tests/unit/goal3-task104-final-replay-request-fail-closed.test.mjs`, `node services/comathd/tests/unit/goal3-task103-campaign-native-final-authority-packaging.test.mjs`, and `node services/comathd/tests/unit/goal3-task102-final-global-replay-authority-producer.test.mjs`. Full `corepack pnpm --dir services/comathd test` exited 0 with Task105 included.
- Residual risk: Goal 3 remains incomplete. Task105 moves target production one native stage earlier and binds it to accepted workstream material, but it still depends on the material artifact already containing a complete Lean project description. It does not yet make earlier planner/agent stages synthesize that material from ordinary candidate/integration outputs, install/configure Lean, fetch mathlib, broaden theorem coverage, provide OS-level sandboxing, validate full Pi/Codex lifecycle behavior, or complete final GA audit.

## Task 117: Append-Only Service-Owned LeanRunManifest Storage

- [x] Confirm no earlier `[ ]`, `[~]`, or `Commit: pending` task item remained before opening Task 117.
- [x] Treat Task116's tracker next step as authoritative and keep the Task117 slice scoped to Lean Authority v3 provenance hardening.

Completion record:

- Work done: extended the LeanRunManifest v3 unit coverage so a second `runServiceOwnedLeanCommandV3()` call with the same `claim_id` and `run_id` must throw `lean_run_manifest_append_only_violation` and leave the original stderr log intact. Hardened `runServiceOwnedLeanCommandV3()` to compute the stdout, stderr, and manifest evidence paths before executing the runner, assert that none already exists, and only then run Lean/fake Lean and write service-owned artifacts. This prevents silent overwrite of prior service-owned Lean evidence for a reused run id. The new gate exposed a live-adapter run-id collision where two campaigns under the same claim could reuse the same `LRUN-*`; `stableRunId()` now binds the run id to campaign, obligation, and candidate identity.
- Verification evidence: TDD RED was observed before implementation: `node services/comathd/tests/unit/goal3-task7-lean-run-manifest-v3.test.mjs` failed with `AssertionError [ERR_ASSERTION]: Missing expected exception` because duplicate `LRUN-0002` overwrote existing evidence instead of throwing. After implementation, `corepack pnpm --dir services/comathd build` exited 0. Focused regressions exited 0: Task7, Task41, Task113, Task114, Task115, and Task116. The first full `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/comathd test` run exposed the live-adapter run-id collision in Task113 (`candidate_blocked` instead of `candidate_failed`); after binding `stableRunId()` to campaign/obligation/candidate identity, Task113 and the full default chain exited 0. Package/root verification gates exited 0: `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/comathd typecheck`, `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/comathd test`, `corepack pnpm build`, `corepack pnpm typecheck`, `corepack pnpm test`, and `git diff --check` with Windows LF-to-CRLF warnings only.
- Residual risk: Goal 3 remains incomplete. Task117 enforces append-only behavior at the service-owned LeanRunManifest writer path, but it does not provide OS-level immutable storage, external tamper-evident audit logs, richer Lean/mathlib dependency fetching, nontrivial theorem synthesis, full Pi/Codex lifecycle validation, or final GA audit.
- Next step: Task118 should continue Lean Authority v3 provenance hardening, preferably by adding a service-owned provenance audit event / manifest index for every LeanRunManifest write or by making promotion-visible final evidence reject stale final replay manifests after artifact rewrite attempts.
- Commit: `d0a3888` (`Make Lean run evidence append-only`)

## Task133: Public Snapshot Download Vs Internal Restore Contract

- [x] Confirm no earlier `[ ]`, `[~]`, or `Commit: pending` task item remained before opening Task133.
- [x] Re-read the Goal 3 required context set and confirm the current repository state instead of relying on prior memory.
- [x] Run bounded read-only audits over snapshot/package download semantics and Pi campaign export public tool results.
- [x] Add a failing service regression proving default public snapshot downloads are sanitized, non-restorable, and cover audit/workstream/artifact blob materials while explicit internal restore snapshots preserve byte-for-byte runtime material.
- [x] Add a failing Pi regression proving `comath.campaign.export` receives defense-in-depth public sanitizer coverage.
- [x] Split snapshot manifests into `public_download` and `internal_restore` contracts, reject restore from public downloads, and preserve internal restore fidelity through explicit `audience: "internal_restore"`.
- [x] Extend public snapshot copy sanitization to audit logs, workstream status/spec/report/graph-patch files, artifact indexes, and UTF-8 artifact blobs.

Completion record:

- Work done: treated Task132's tracker next step as authoritative and hardened the public-vs-internal snapshot/package contract. Added `goal3-task133-public-snapshot-restore-contract.test.mjs`; RED showed default `exportSnapshot()` had no `snapshot_kind` and still advertised `can_restore: true` even when public copies could be sanitized. Implemented `snapshot_kind: "public_download" | "internal_restore"` and `audience?: "public_download" | "internal_restore"`, made public downloads the default, set `can_restore=false` for public downloads, and made `restoreSnapshot()` fail closed with `SNAPSHOT_PUBLIC_DOWNLOAD_NOT_RESTORABLE` unless the manifest is an explicit internal restore snapshot. Public snapshot copies now sanitize audit logs, workstream files, artifact indexes, and UTF-8 artifact blobs in addition to the earlier evidence/gate/candidate/agent-output files; non-text artifact blobs remain hash-preserved. Updated restore-fidelity callers and tests to request `audience: "internal_restore"`. Added `goal3-task133-campaign-export-public-sanitizer.test.mjs` and wrapped Pi `comath.campaign.export` through `publicToolResult()` for defense-in-depth against stale or future server-side export fields.
- Verification evidence: TDD RED was observed before implementation: service Task133 failed on missing `snapshot_kind` / public download contract, and Pi Task133 failed because `comath.campaign.export` leaked `lean_kernel_clean_replay`, `formal_replay_passed`, `completed_formal_proof`, `formally_checked`, and `proven` from a fake client body. After implementation, focused regressions exited 0: Task131 public download consumer sanitizer, Task133 public snapshot restore contract, Phase16 snapshot/replay, Task17 GA acceptance workflow, Task130 Pi snapshot/replay public sanitizer, Task133 Pi campaign export public sanitizer, and Phase18 GA snapshot replay integration. - [x] Confirm no earlier `[ ]`, `[~]`, or `Commit: pending` task item remained before opening Task140.

Completion record:

- Work done: treated Task139's tracker next step as authoritative and audited the Pi extension consumer side of the Task139 public snapshot export contract. Found that public `/snapshot/export` now returns project-relative `manifest_path` values, but Pi `comath.snapshot.verify`, `comath.snapshot.restore`, `comath.replay.verifyManifest`, and `/cm:snapshot verify|restore|verify-manifest` still did not advertise or forward `project_root`, forcing callers back toward host absolute paths or failed relative resolution. Hardened Pi tool schemas and execution bodies to accept optional `project_root`, made `/cm:snapshot` default to the runtime `project_root` for verify/restore/replay-manifest flows, advertised `verify-manifest`, and kept absolute manifest inputs compatible.
- Verification evidence: `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/pi-extension build` exited 0. TDD RED was observed before implementation: `node extensions/comath-pi/tests/goal3-task140-pi-relative-snapshot-manifest-contract.test.mjs` failed because `comath.snapshot.verify` did not advertise `project_root`; after the first fix, the same test failed because `/cm:snapshot` did not advertise `verify-manifest`. After implementation, focused regressions exited 0: Task140 Pi relative snapshot manifest contract, Task131 Pi snapshot runtime consistency, Phase59 Pi product surface tool routing, and Task130 Pi snapshot/replay public authority sanitizer. Package gates exited 0: `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/pi-extension typecheck` and `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/pi-extension test`, with Task140 discovered by the default runner. `node scripts/phase0-smoke.mjs` exited 0 with 33 required entries and 33 invariants. `git diff --check` exited 0 with Windows LF-to-CRLF warnings only, and `Test-Path -LiteralPath .comath` returned `False`.

Completion record:

- Work done: treated Task156's next step as authoritative and selected the smallest safe durable-session slice: service-owned operator-session manifest persistence rather than indefinite WebSocket/SSE transport, OS-level isolation, broad Lean replay, or full guided real-Pi execution. The service writes `.comath/release/pi-codex-lifecycle/<session_id>/operator-session-manifest.json`, binds artifact refs by project-relative path/hash/size, computes the next lifecycle route from completed steps, records `release.pi_codex_lifecycle_operator_session_persisted`, and returns explicit non-authority fields: `durable_transport_provided: false`, `pi_direct_write_allowed: false`, `direct_trusted_state_mutation: false`, `proof_authority: "none"`, `can_promote_claim: false`, and `can_certify_ga: false`.
- Code-review hardening: read-only review found that raw `project_id` could leak before validation, actor/cursor fields could expose secrets, audit could store raw actor values, and a poisoned existing `created_at` could be trusted. Added regression coverage for those cases and fixed validation ordering, secret/proof-vocabulary scrubbing, audit actor sanitization, and strict ISO-millis `created_at` fallback before commit.
- Verification evidence: `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/comathd build` exited 0. TDD RED was observed before implementation: `node services/comathd/tests/unit/goal3-task157-pi-codex-operator-session-persistence.test.mjs` failed because `../../dist/index.js` did not export `persistPiCodexLifecycleOperatorSession`. A later leak-regression RED was observed before review fixes: the focused Task157 test failed because the session result exposed `OPENAI_API_KEY=plain-token` / `Authorization: Bearer plain-token`. After implementation and review fixes, focused Task157 exited 0. Adjacent regressions exited 0: Task148 Pi/Codex lifecycle evidence intake, Task149 durable service lifecycle probe, Task150 Codex API account/network probe, Task152 real-Pi install/runtime-registration probe, and Task146 Pi/Codex lifecycle readiness. Package gates exited 0: `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/comathd typecheck` and `corepack pnpm --filter @comath/comathd test`, with Task157 discovered by the default comathd runner. Post-doc gates exited 0: `node scripts/phase0-smoke.mjs` with 33 required entries and 33 invariants, `git diff --check` with Windows LF-to-CRLF warnings only, and `Test-Path -LiteralPath .comath` returned `False`.

It never executes Nix/Firejail/sandbox-exec tools, inspects store/profile/sandbox-policy state, records versions, writes PATH entries, executable paths, caller paths, or raw caller diagnostics into manifests. Caller-supplied platform strings, tool paths, capability/kernel facts, success booleans, proof-authority fields, and GA claims remain untrusted and are not accepted. The resulting manifests still keep `adapter_execution_isolation.current_boundary="process_boundary_only"`, `os_enforced=false`, `claims_runtime_enforcement=false`, `proof_authority="none"`, `can_promote_claim=false`, and `can_certify_ga=false`. Adapter OS-isolation readiness review still rejects host capability manifests; only canonical service-owned OS-isolation probe/evidence artifacts and readiness reviews can feed release gates.

Task196 does not ship production OCI/Nix/Firejail/Windows AppContainer/macOS sandbox helper binaries, does not execute helpers under OS-enforced isolation, does not make host facility metadata readiness evidence, does not provide durable long-lived operator transport, does not broaden Lean/mathlib replay, does not complete fully interactive real-Pi execution, and does not certify GA.

## Task215: Final Replay DependencyClosureV2 Binding

- [x] Re-read live Goal 3 tracker state, current repo status, Task214 residual frontier, clean replay/dependency-closure code, final replay manifest code, and external Lean supply-chain docs before selecting the next frontier.
- [x] Use bounded read-only subagent review for Task215 frontier selection across DependencyClosureV2 final replay wiring vs OCI/provider-helper work while keeping the main thread as the only writer.
- [x] Add a failing focused Task215 regression proving final clean replay writes V2 dependency closure material into the stable `dependency_closure.json` artifact slot, fails closed on untracked imports even when Lean/Lake stdout exits successfully, and binds package revision material into FinalReplayManifest v3 dependency locks.
- [x] Implement final replay `DependencyClosureV2` binding, expose the capability, keep report paths stable, and treat Lean/Lake toolchain modules such as `Lake`, `Init`, and `Std` as toolchain modules rather than external theorem dependencies.
- [x] Run focused Task215, adjacent Task10/102/103/214 regressions, smoke, comathd build/typecheck/test, root test, runtime-state verification, diff-check, and review gates.

- Work done: selected the final replay dependency-closure frontier after Task214, with both read-only subagents agreeing that this outranked OCI/provider-helper work for Task215. Added `goal3-task215-final-replay-dependency-closure-v2-binding.test.mjs`; added `final_replay_dependency_closure_v2_binding`; changed `runCleanLeanReplay()` to call `checkDependencyClosureV2()` while keeping the stable `dependency_closure.json` artifact path; bound V2 Lake manifest package revision material into FinalReplayManifest v3 `dependency_lock.external_revisions`; classified `Lake`, `Init`, and `Std` as Lean/Lake toolchain modules; tightened project-local import handling so missing `MathResearch.*` modules cannot pass by prefix alone; and synchronized README, AGENTS, TODO, threat model, external Lean supply-chain docs, GA release criteria, phase0 smoke, REVIEW, and this tracker.
- Boundary hardening: final clean replay now emits `comath.dependency_closure.v2` dependency evidence and can fail final replay on untracked imports, missing local project imports, local Mathlib shadowing, symlink escapes, untrusted dependencies, missing pins, and unknown package licenses even if Lean/Lake command output appears successful. The V2 package list is also bound into the v3 dependency lock so final replay packaging carries package revision provenance. The existing `checkDependencyClosure()` compatibility API remains available for legacy non-final reports and tests, but service-owned final clean replay uses V2.
- Verification evidence: TDD RED was observed before implementation when focused Task215 failed because final replay dependency closure had no V2 schema (`undefined !== "comath.dependency_closure.v2"`). After initial global V2 wiring, adjacent Task102 and Task103 failed closed; systematic debugging traced the root cause to `lakefile.lean`'s `import Lake` being parsed as an untracked theorem dependency, so `DependencyClosureV2` now classifies `Lake`, `Init`, and `Std` as Lean/Lake toolchain modules. A later self-review RED caught a local-project import bypass where `MathResearch.Missing` was not reported as untracked because the old V2 rule allowed the `MathResearch` prefix by itself; V2 now requires local project imports to resolve to clean-workspace modules unless a trusted package or Lean/Lake toolchain module provides them. - Work done: selected the Pi/operator lease binding frontier after bounded read-only exploration. Gauss recommended concrete OCI/Docker-style external provider-helper integration as a larger next frontier; Dalton recommended binding Pi/Codex operator transport leases to existing service-owned AgentRun log-session primitives; Aquinas recommended an optional bounded real installed-toolchain Mathlib smoke only when the host environment is prepared. The main thread chose Dalton's smaller product-trust gap for Task220. Added `goal3-task220-pi-operator-transport-lease-agentrun-log-session-binding.test.mjs`; added `pi_codex_operator_transport_lease_agentrun_log_session_binding`; introduced `PiCodexLifecycleOperatorTransportLogSessionBinding`; parsed requested lease routes as `/agent/run/<run-id>/log-session`; required the requested lease route to match the recovery checkpoint's canonical route; proved readability through `formatAgentRunLogSseSession()` before lease persistence; persisted canonical route/run/cursor/event-count/body-hash provenance on bounded lease artifacts; added audit-event binding fields; and hardened guided real-Pi execution lease consumption so old, expired, wrong-recovery, or tampered lease artifacts fail closed unless they rebind to current service-owned AgentRun log-session material. Task162, Task164, and Task166 fixtures now create real service-owned AgentRuns instead of relying on synthetic `RUN-*` route text. Synchronized README, AGENTS, TODO, threat model, GA release criteria, phase0 smoke, REVIEW, and this tracker.
- Boundary hardening: Task220 closes the route-text ambiguity where bounded leases could preserve sanitized but arbitrary `/agent/run/.../log-session` strings without proving the run existed, belonged to the project, matched the recovery checkpoint, or was readable through the service-owned log-session path. Lease creation now rejects missing, expired-at-consumption, stale, wrong-project, wrong-recovery, or unreadable AgentRun routes before writing or consuming the lease artifact, canonicalizes secret-bearing/success-shaped route text to the bound service route, and records only non-authoritative provenance. Guided execution also rejects pre-Task220 lease artifacts that lack the binding and re-runs service-owned log-session formatting to catch tampered body hashes, cursors, event counts, completion flags, or route/run/project fields. The binding carries `proof_authority="none"`, `can_promote_claim=false`, `durable_transport_provided=false`, and `long_lived_sse_provided=false`; it is not proof authority, not durable transport, not an indefinite SSE/WebSocket session, not OS isolation, and not GA certification.

Added `goal3-task250-pi-unattended-real-host-execution-attempt-consumer.test.mjs`; added `comath.release.piCodexLifecycleUnattendedRealHostExecutionAttempt`; added `/cm:release lifecycle-unattended-real-host-execution-attempt`; added the `lifecycle-unattended-real-host-execution-attempt` read-only planner checkpoint between readiness and Codex API probe/review; updated runtime registration subcommands, Phase6/Phase26 exposure guards, Task223 planner expectations, Pi package test discovery, phase0 smoke discovery, GA release criteria, README, AGENTS, TODO, adapter contracts, threat model, REVIEW, and this tracker.

The default product path therefore records a replayable executor-unavailable blocker unless a future service-owned executor configuration path is added. Public Pi output forces proof, GA, durable/live transport, direct-Pi-write, direct-trusted-state, executor-invoked, execution-attempt-success, and unattended-completion flags false.

- Commit: `baf104a` (`Add goal3 task274 live retrieval suggestion repair`)
- Work done: selected the live retrieval suggestion-to-repair frontier after Task273 because the product could record opt-in live retrieval results but still could not use a bounded quoted Lean suggestion to repair a rejected placeholder-free candidate. Added `goal3-task274-pi-goal-mode-live-retrieval-suggestion-repair.test.mjs`; extended live Jina result summaries to extract fenced Lean/Lean4 snippets only after prompt-injection scan pass; persisted bounded suggestion metadata with SHA-256 hashes and no-authority flags; extended repair execution with `live_retrieval_exact_statement_lean_suggestion` so exact same locked theorem/lemma statements can be materialized from retrieval suggestions without treating them as proof; and changed LeanRunner run-id derivation to include the Lean file hash so repaired reruns write new append-only manifests rather than overwriting rejected evidence.
